title: Fire containment device
abstract: Provided herein are fire containment devices having a bag, a bag closure for selectively obstructing a bag opening to prevent fluid communication between a bag internal cavity and an ambient environment, a bag fastener configured to maintain the bag closure in the closed state when a pressure in the bag internal cavity is below a bag failure pressure, and a vent extending through an aperture in the bag wall, the vent including a vent inlet exposed to the bag internal cavity and a vent outlet exposed to the ambient environment to permit a flow of off-gas material to pass therethrough in a direction from the vent inlet to the vent outlet, the vent configured to maintain pressure in the bag internal cavity below bag failure pressure and flow of off-gas material exiting the vent at a flammability density below a lower flammability limit of the off-gas material.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A fire containment device, comprising: a bag including: a bag wall defining a bag internal cavity and a bag opening; a bag closure transitionable between a closed state, in which the bag closure obstructs the bag opening to prevent fluid communication between the bag internal cavity and an ambient environment, and an open state, in which the bag closure permits fluid communication between the bag internal cavity and the ambient environment via the bag opening; a bag fastener configured to maintain the bag closure in the closed state when a pressure in the bag internal cavity is below a bag failure pressure; a vent extending through an aperture in the bag wall, the vent including a vent inlet exposed to the bag internal cavity and a vent outlet exposed to the ambient environment; wherein the vent is configured to permit a flow of an off-gas material to pass therethrough in a direction from the vent inlet to the vent outlet during a fire event in which the bag closure is in the closed state and a combustible object stored in the bag internal cavity is combusting; wherein the vent is configured such that the flow of the off-gas material passing therethrough has a flow rate sufficient to maintain the pressure in the bag internal cavity below the bag failure pressure during the fire event; and wherein the vent is configured such that the flow of the off-gas material exiting the vent outlet during the fire event has a flammability density that is below a lower flammability limit (LFL) of the off-gas material. 2. The fire containment device of claim 1, wherein the fire containment device excludes a fire suppression material. 3. The fire containment device of claim 1, wherein the vent includes a filter between the vent inlet and the vent outlet; and wherein the filter is configured to reduce the flammability density of the flow of offgas material as the flow passes through the filter. 4. The fire containment device of claim 3, wherein the filter includes a filter body and a filter flange extending from the filter body; wherein the vent further includes a filter mount configured to fix the filter to the bag wall; wherein the filter mount includes an exterior clamping plate having an exterior clamping plate aperture and an interior clamping plate having an interior clamping plate aperture; and wherein the exterior clamping plate aperture and the interior clamping plate aperture are shaped and sized to accept the filter body therethrough and to not accept the filter flange therethrough. 5. The fire containment device of claim 4, wherein the exterior and interior clamping plates each include at least one fastener aperture sized to receive a fastener therethrough for compressing the filter flange and the bag wall together between the exterior and interior clamping plates. 6. The fire containment device of claim 3, wherein the vent inlet is a first vent inlet, the vent outlet is a first vent outlet, and the filter is a first filter; wherein the vent further includes a second vent inlet, a second vent outlet, and a second filter between the second vent inlet and the second vent outlet; wherein the first filter is configured to reduce the flammability density of a first portion of the flow of off-gas material as the first portion passes through the first filter; and wherein the second filter is configured to reduce the flammability density of a second portion of the flow of off-gas material as the second portion passes through the second filter. 7. The fire containment device of claim 4, wherein the filter flange is at least one of sealed and welded to the bag wall. 8. The fire containment device of claim 3, wherein the filter is a respirator filter oriented to permit flow of the off-gas material therethrough in the direction from the vent inlet to the vent outlet during the fire event in which the bag closure is in the closed state and the combustible object stored in the bag internal cavity is combusting. 9. The fire containment device of claim 1, wherein the combustible object stored in the bag internal cavity is a battery. 10. The fire containment device of claim 9, wherein the combustible object stored in the bag internal cavity is a lithium-ion battery. 11. The fire containment device of claim 10, wherein the lithium-ion battery is for at least one of an e-bike and an e-scooter. 12. The fire containment device of claim 1, wherein the bag closure is configured to permit a charging cord to extend through the bag opening when the bag closure is in the closed state. 13. The fire containment device of claim 1, where is configured such that a ratio between a volume of the bag internal cavity and an electrical energy of the component stored therein is at least .015 cubic inches per watt-hour. 14. The fire containment device of claim 1, wherein the bag wall is non-rigid. 15. The fire containment device of claim 1, wherein the bag wall is flame-resistant. 16. The fire containment device of claim 1, wherein the bag wall is gas-impermeable. 17. The fire containment device of claim 1, wherein the bag wall includes at least a first bag wall panel fixed to a second bag wall panel to define a seam of the bag wall. 18. The fire containment device of claim 17, wherein the first bag wall panel is fixed to the second bag wall panel via at least one of stitching, adhesive, and welding. 19. The fire containment device of claim 1, wherein the bag wall includes at least an interior layer defining the bag internal cavity and an exterior layer exposed to the ambient environment. 20. The fire containment device of claim 19, wherein the interior layer is a fiber mat material. 21. The fire containment device of claim 19, wherein the exterior layer is a fiber textile substrate being at least one of coated or impregnated with silicone. 22. The fire containment device of claim 1, wherein the bag closure includes at least one of a latch, a buckle, a strap, a hook and loop fastener, and a zipper.
